title: Multiply decimals (1&2-digit factors)
description: Multiply numbers with tenths and hundredths like 3.1x3.3 or 1.7x0.12

hints:
$6 \times 36$ and $0.6 \times 0.36$ multiply the same digits in the same order.  

So, the product of both problems will also have the same digits in the same order. 

Let's multiply $6 \times 36$.  Then we can estimate to place the decimal point in the product of $0.6 \times 0.36$.

Let's estimate to place the decimal in $0.6\times 0.36$.

$\begin{align}
0.6 \times 0.36 &\approx 0.6\times 0.4\\\\
\phantom{0.6 \times 0.36} &\approx \dfrac6{10}\times \dfrac4{10}\\\\
\phantom{0.6 \times 0.36} &\approx \dfrac{24}{100}\\\\
&\approx 0.24
\end{align}$

Where can we place the decimal in $216$ to get a product close to $0.24$?

&\approx \dfrac{4}{10}\times \dfrac{6}{10}\\\\

\end{align}$
